back_translation: For the choirmaster. According to Jeduthun. A Psalm of Asaph.
I cried out to God; I cried aloud to God so that He would hear me.
In the day of trouble I sought the Lord; at night I stretched out my hands in prayer without ceasing, but my soul refused to be comforted.
I remembered You, O God, and I groaned; I mused until my spirit grew faint. Selah
You kept my eyes from closing; I was too troubled to speak.
I considered the days of old, the years long past.
At night I remembered my song; I mused in my heart, and my spirit pondered:
'Will the Lord reject us forever and never show His favor again?
Has His steadfast love ceased forever? Has His promise failed for all time?
Has God forgotten to be gracious? Has He in anger shut off His compassion?' Selah
Then I said, 'This is my grief: that the right hand of the Most High has changed.'
I will remember the works of the LORD; I will remember Your wonders of old.
I will meditate on all Your works and ponder Your mighty deeds.
O God, Your way is holy. What god is as great as our God?
You are the God who works wonders; You display Your strength among the peoples.
With Your arm You redeemed Your people, the sons of Jacob and Joseph. Selah
O God, when the waters saw You, the waters saw You and trembled; even the depths shook.
The clouds poured down water; the skies resounded with thunder; Your arrows flashed back and forth.
Your thunder resounded in the whirlwind; the lightning lit up the world; the earth trembled and quaked.
Your way went through the sea, Your path through the mighty waters, but no one could find Your footprints.
You led Your people like a flock by the hand of Moses and Aaron.
